title: Provincial Nominee Program: Non-Express Entry Process - Get your documents ready - Canada.ca
content:
## Get your documents ready
As the principal applicant, you’ll sign for the entire application, including those of your family members.
- Forms you’ll fill out in the portal You’ll fill out these forms directly in the portal (for yourself and any family members 18 or older): Generic Application Form for Canada (IMM 0008) Schedule A – Background/Declaration (IMM 5669) Additional Family Information (IMM 5406) Supplementary Information - Your travels (IMM 5562)

## Forms you’ll fill out in the portal
You’ll fill out these forms directly in the portal (for yourself and any family members 18 or older):
- Generic Application Form for Canada (IMM 0008)
- Schedule A – Background/Declaration (IMM 5669)
- Additional Family Information (IMM 5406)
- Supplementary Information - Your travels (IMM 5562)
- Forms you’ll download and fill out Download, fill out and upload these PDF forms to your online application: Document Checklist (IMM 0199) Schedule 4: Economic Classes: Provincial Nominees (IMM 0008 SCHEDULE 4) Schedule 4A: Economic Classes: Provincial Nominees – Business Nominees [IMM 0008 SCHEDULE 4A] You don’t need to sign these forms.

## Forms you’ll download and fill out
Download, fill out and upload these PDF forms to your online application:
- Document Checklist (IMM 0199)
- Schedule 4: Economic Classes: Provincial Nominees (IMM 0008 SCHEDULE 4)
- Schedule 4A: Economic Classes: Provincial Nominees – Business Nominees [IMM 0008 SCHEDULE 4A]
You don’t need to sign these forms.
- Forms you’ll fill out if they apply to you Print, fill out and sign these forms by hand (and get the third party to sign them by hand also). You’ll then upload them to your online application: Declaration From Non-accompanying Parent/Guardian for Minors Immigrating to Canada [IMM 5604] Statutory Declaration of Common-law Union [IMM 5409] Use of a representative [IMM 5476] You need to sign these forms.

## Forms you’ll fill out if they apply to you
Print, fill out and sign these forms by hand (and get the third party to sign them by hand also). You’ll then upload them to your online application:
- Declaration From Non-accompanying Parent/Guardian for Minors Immigrating to Canada [IMM 5604]
- Statutory Declaration of Common-law Union [IMM 5409]
- Use of a representative [IMM 5476]
You need to sign these forms.
- Documents to include in your application Unless we tell you otherwise, all supporting documents must be in English or French . Photos You need one photo taken within 12 months for each person on your application. Follow the instructions in the online application to scan and upload both sides of your photo. Confirmation of nomination Submit the nomination confirmation from the province or territory that nominated you. Depending on the province or territory, this could be an approval letter or another official notice. Travel documents and passports Submit copies of the passport or travel document pages for you and your family that show passport number, issue date, expiry date photo, name, date and place of birth any pages showing changes to name, birth date, or expiration You must have a valid regular passport. Diplomatic, official, service, or public affairs passports aren’t valid for Canada immigration. If you live outside your country of nationality, also include a visa copy for your current country of residence. Identity and civil status Please provide the following for you and your spouse or common-law partner: birth certificates for you and your spouse or partner any legal documents showing changes to name or date of birth (if applicable) marriage certificates, and final divorce or annulment certificates If you have been married more than once, include documents for each marriage and each divorce or annulment. death certificates for former spouses or common-law partners (if applicable) national IDs or family/household registry book (if applicable) If you have a common-law partner complete and sign the Statutory Declaration of Common-Law Union [IMM 5409] provide evidence of at least 12 consecutive months of cohabitation (for example, joint bank statements, leases, utility bills) Documents must list the names of both common-law partners. both you and your partner must read and sign the declaration by hand Children’s information (if applicable) Please provide birth certificates showing parents’ names proof of full custody for children under 18 proof that children can leave the court’s jurisdiction legally approved adoption papers from recognized authorities for adopted children If the other parent is not coming to Canada, also include a copy of the Declaration from Non-accompanying Parent/Guardian for Minors Immigrating to Canada [IMM 5604] form for each child a copy of the non-accompanying parent’s photo ID with signature If your child’s age was locked in before August 1, 2014, submit proof of continuous full-time studies for children aged 22 or older including complete school records/transcripts since age 22 letters from schools showing class hours per day and days attended per week proof of full financial support by parents since age 22 Police certificate You must provide a police certificate from any country (except Canada) in which you spent 6 months in a row or more since the age of 18. In some countries, it can take a long time to get a police certificate. Therefore, you may want to get it early. If you have a criminal record, you may not be allowed to enter Canada. You can’t enter Canada if you pose a risk to Canada’s security. If you can’t provide the required documents Please upload a letter explaining everything you did to try to get them. If it will take time to get some documents, please also tell us when you expect to receive them.

## Documents to include in your application
Unless we tell you otherwise, all supporting documents must be in English or French .
You need one photo taken within 12 months for each person on your application. Follow the instructions in the online application to scan and upload both sides of your photo.
Submit the nomination confirmation from the province or territory that nominated you. Depending on the province or territory, this could be an approval letter or another official notice.
Submit copies of the passport or travel document pages for you and your family that show
- passport number, issue date, expiry date
- photo, name, date and place of birth
- any pages showing changes to name, birth date, or expiration
You must have a valid regular passport. Diplomatic, official, service, or public affairs passports aren’t valid for Canada immigration. If you live outside your country of nationality, also include a visa copy for your current country of residence.
Please provide the following for you and your spouse or common-law partner:
- birth certificates for you and your spouse or partner
- any legal documents showing changes to name or date of birth (if applicable)
- marriage certificates, and final divorce or annulment certificates If you have been married more than once, include documents for each marriage and each divorce or annulment.
- If you have been married more than once, include documents for each marriage and each divorce or annulment.
- death certificates for former spouses or common-law partners (if applicable)
- national IDs or family/household registry book (if applicable)
If you have a common-law partner
- complete and sign the Statutory Declaration of Common-Law Union [IMM 5409]
- provide evidence of at least 12 consecutive months of cohabitation (for example, joint bank statements, leases, utility bills) Documents must list the names of both common-law partners.
- Documents must list the names of both common-law partners.
- both you and your partner must read and sign the declaration by hand
- birth certificates showing parents’ names
- proof of full custody for children under 18
- proof that children can leave the court’s jurisdiction
- legally approved adoption papers from recognized authorities for adopted children
If the other parent is not coming to Canada, also include
- a copy of the Declaration from Non-accompanying Parent/Guardian for Minors Immigrating to Canada [IMM 5604] form for each child
- a copy of the non-accompanying parent’s photo ID with signature
If your child’s age was locked in before August 1, 2014, submit proof of continuous full-time studies for children aged 22 or older including
- complete school records/transcripts since age 22
- letters from schools showing class hours per day and days attended per week
- proof of full financial support by parents since age 22
You must provide a police certificate from any country (except Canada) in which you spent 6 months in a row or more since the age of 18. In some countries, it can take a long time to get a police certificate. Therefore, you may want to get it early. If you have a criminal record, you may not be allowed to enter Canada. You can’t enter Canada if you pose a risk to Canada’s security.

### If you can’t provide the required documents
Please upload a letter explaining everything you did to try to get them. If it will take time to get some documents, please also tell us when you expect to receive them.

## Check your signatures
If you are missing signatures, your application will be returned without being processed.
- Check all forms for signature requirements.
- Sign in the correct places and ensure the correct person signs.
- Make sure that your signature matches the name on your passport.
- Some forms need multiple signatures or signatures from more than one person.
- Fill in all required dates.
- A parent or legal guardian must sign for any child under 18 when required.
Print all pages in this section
